description_long: This is a compact, vintage-style biplane with a bright orange and red color scheme. It features two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, supported by struts, and a single propeller mounted at the nose. The fuselage is rounded and tapers toward the tail, which has a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and appears to be a single-seat, open design. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel, typical of a taildragger configuration. Rivets and panel lines are visible along the fuselage, suggesting a metal construction.
description: A small, orange-red vintage biplane with stacked wings, a single nose propeller, and a classic taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stacked biplane wings, central propeller, and the rounded nose.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wing struts, cockpit placement, and the tailwheel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the tapering fuselage.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the propeller, wing structure, and landing gear details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a small tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit integrated near the nose of the fuselage.
